entry.title: Re: Embedded PPDS Setup Matrix
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu

Thank you for the excellent documentation, I have some questions regarding the Manual Setup matrix creation and Campaign Planning:

How many setup groups you can to create for resource?

How many setup transitions the transaction Create Setup Matrix in - /n/SAPAPO/CDPSC7 allow to create?

In the transacción OP43, we load all the setup groups,  How many setup groups you can to create in this transaction?

Example: If I have a recipe with 5 operations and each operation has different resources, I will have different preparation matrices and setup groups for each phase of operation.

How do I link each configuration group with the campaign profile?

I understand that the campaign profile will have location, max. number of charges, campaign color, and Production planner (to limit which products will be planned with campaigns), but I'm not clear if I should create a campaign profile for each setup group.

I appreciate your valuable response.

Best regards

Darsy

entry.title: Re: Embedded PPDS Setup Matrix
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu

I have other question:

Why you define 2 time =1 hr, for setup phase? in the step :"Now create the master data like material master, BOM, Recipe, PDS. Here additional step is we need to assign the setup matrix to recipe at operation level for each material. You can add only to setup operations in recipe."

I appreciate your valuable response.

Best regards

Darsy

entry.title: Re: API for add new Pricing Conditions to an Existing Pur...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Have you tried setting the validity date of your old pricing condition on the day you want to create new condition? Validity date of your old pricing cond. may block you from creating the new one under the same inforecord number. It should work if you update in your inforecord first the validity date and then create new pricing condition. 

Please let me know what you think about it so we may try to discuss it further.

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: Factory calendar
entry.content:
Hello @ZZ3987 

Factory Calendars are pre-delivered by SAP in S/4HANA Public cloud. You cannot create entirely new calendars directly via the frontend, but you can adjust usage by assigning existing calendars or requesting custom calendars through SAP if needed.

Refere to SAP References below,

SAP Community How to Maintain Factory Calendars in S/4HANA Cloud

SAP Help Factory Calendar

Let me know if it helps

Thank you, and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: A Step-By-Step Walkthrough for SAP Analytics Cloud compass (PART II)
entry.content:
Thank you for the detail blog. I am stuck to get many drivers in one scenario. I can right click a value cell and navigate to "Create Compass Simulation", but this provides only one driver that is tie to the cell value. I am unable to add additional drivers in Compass. Also, in story canvas page (Optimized Design Experience), I do not see Insert → Simulation → Compass Menu.

Thank you

Jai

entry.title: Type for input parameter for XML string
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am trying to define a function module input parameter which will be evaluated to an XML string. Eventually, a web service will be created from this FM.

Can I use the type STRING for the input parameter?

Regards,

Konstantinos

entry.title: Re: Assign the appropriate business catalogs or roles to...
entry.content:
Hello, 

If you used CDS Views it should have roles defined in this help documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/5418de55938d1d22e10000000a44147b.html?locale=en-US

Please search for CDS taht you used and assign required roles to the users. Sometimes CDS might require basic roles for certain transaction. Exmaple here: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/992295f817e04555b385b44b97123ee2.html?locale=en-US

Hope it will help you!

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Bringing federal Public Sector cloud solutions to life for SAP America
entry.content:
A new digital foundation for the US federal government

SAP provides commitment and energy to support US federal government agencies as they meet the challenges of today and innovate for tomorrow. Learn how a cross-functional Industry team demonstrated that SAP’s innovations and solutions can drive government efficiency, by creating premier content and experiences tailored to this unique time in the federal government landscape.

Meeting this moment

Today, the US government faces a digital turning point. Fragmented systems hinder the very operations that underpin national security and economic strength – from hiring and workforce management to budgeting, travel, logistics and mission delivery. These aren’t background tasks – they’re frontline levers of national competitiveness. When they fail, progress slows, costs rise, and force readiness suffers.

For a generation, SAP has been a partner to US government agencies in the Department of Defense, Federal Civilian and Security arenas. SAP’s NS2 is our independent, US national subsidiary that holds 7+ security authorizations and has earned 8 consecutive superior ratings from the Defense Counterintelligence & Security Agency – an honor held by only 5% of cleared organizations.

SAP is ready to re-write America’s future with secure, mission-ready AI. Our US-based Industry Advisory met the moment, quarterbacking a regional approach to activities ranging from brand voice and summit meetings, to partnering agreements and investments, to demand generation and network building at an industry-specific SAP NOW event in Washington DC this June 12th.

The US Industry Advisory team in collaboration with the IBU Public Sector positioned SAP America in alignment with the administration’s priorities for a secure and more efficient government. They articulated how our US investments, innovation and workforce expansion fuel American economic growth. For example, SAP America plans to double US GDP contribution from $100B to $200B by 2029, simultaneously creating 400,000 new US jobs in the SAP ecosystem.  

"SAP is built to help the largest, most complex organizations move faster, work smarter, and deliver more, while keeping safety and security first. This is our chance to build a stronger, AI-enabled, and more secure digital foundation that fuels impact today and trust for decades to come."

Michele Hovet, North American Industry Advisory, Public Services, Healthcare and Utilities 

Firing the customer imagination

SAP is the only technology company that combines decades of mission-critical experience, deep understanding of government operations, and AI-powered tools that are already trusted by 90% of the Fortune 2000. By taking the bold choice to reinvent with SAP as a trusted partner, the U.S. government can operate with the same AI-powered speed, precision, and agility as the world’s top businesses – without compromising on security, scale, or compliance.

Making vision tangible is where the SAP Lab’s SIX team comes in. You know them from the Business Unleashed Experience Journey for Business Suite. This immersive perspective imagines how customers achieve transformation using a combination of industry-specific applications, a harmonized foundation of business-ready data, and AI delivering faster time-to-value. Seizing this chance to pilot an end-to-end experience journey for the Public Sector, colleagues teamed up to produce the Federal Innovation Showcase.

Well-received at the event, this showcase follows a program manager seeking to accelerate their project within highly regulated boundaries under the oversight of a Deputy Secretary, and participation from the Chief Finance Officer, Chief Procurement Officer and Chief Human Capital Officer. Our storyline can be used in customer meetings to illustrate Agentic AI and integrated data-based insights. Viewers immediately ‘got it’ and delighted in walking across processes with the help of our AI copilot.

"The SAP Business Suite is the only comprehensive solution designed to modernize how the whole of government truly works at the operational level. Built on decades of federal experience and proven at scale (across civilian and defense), SAP supports embedded AI and automation for real-time decisions and efficiency. We can show agencies that they can act with confidence, maximize value, and respond faster – from the command center to the frontlines."

Andreas Wiedemann, SIX Portfolio Management Center of Excellence

Enduring value attainment

Real transformation requires a unified, secure, and intelligent foundation. One that can operate in real time with AI-powered insight, not after-action reporting. It must scale across government from central agencies to field operations. Security and compliance need to be embedded by design, producing results that withstand public, legislative, and mission scrutiny. This suite solution can then produce strategic awareness and support mission execution across finance, procurement, HR and logistics.

SAP’s IBU Public Sector Global Advisory team targets new and accelerated investment in solutions, environments, enablement and integrated demo capabilities that support the Public Sector customer journey onto cloud suite products. They support regions with tools that enable customer conversations, and tactics to secure each journey. The goal is to produce enduring value for the customer, and industry-leading excellence from SAP.

Called upon to help with this American challenge, IBU colleagues translated the technical and process specificity of the federal audience into a range of new assets. These include solution videos that speak to SAP’s capabilities and introduce us to the new contacts who our regional colleagues are meeting in this moment of change. Transformation at scale can be daunting: each video presents one process to show how government can hire, buy, budget, and deliver more effectively. 

"Across every mission and industry – from HR to finance and supply chain to citizen services – SAP helps government solve big problems in a big way. The challenge is real, but so is the opportunity: to modernize the digital backbone of government and unlock a new era of AI-enabled intelligence, efficiency, and mission continuity."

Martin Klein, IBU Public Sector, Global Industries and Innovation Experience 

84% of government organizations will prioritize investments in data intelligence

90B cyber events happen each week against a city like Washington DC, threatening people, data and assets

The US Administration needs systems that deliver proof, not promises

American officials aren’t short on priorities. The goals are clear: increase competitiveness, reduce waste, and deliver visible public value. SAP’s Industry teams can lead that structural change by consolidating fragmented IT into a unified, AI-powered cloud platform that both advances national readiness and the goals of the US Executive Branch.

 

SAP groups who contributed to this Industry event include: Strategy & Operation's Advisory and Marketing teams; SAP America's Industry, Customer and Solution Advisory teams; US Public Services; SAP NS2's Customer, Product, and Security & Compliance teams; SIX IBU Industry Advisors, GTM, Learning, and Showcase teams

entry.title: Re: Maintain Ship-to-party parameter in SAP PO Template
entry.content:
Hello @Dylan2 

To change your company’s address on PO print templates:

  • Update the Plant Address (SSCUI 101017) or Company Code Address, based on how the form is built.
  • In standard PO forms, $.ShipToParty comes from the Plant Address.
  • If the form uses custom fields, check the CDS View or Form Data Source to confirm the source.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: In Public Cloud, can I generate Defect automatically...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please check the SAP documentation regarding API for Defects:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0629fd486efa4d339f8b10603b6f1e3f/90333b5a5c754a7b893edb25c1de6c62.html?locale=en-US

And here you can find an API that might help you to create a single result: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0629fd486efa4d339f8b10603b6f1e3f/e47b1e2f23974a8c8d0cf1dcc309c5e5.html?locale=en-US

Hope it will help you!

Reagrds, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite trial: Deploy of Integration Fl...
entry.content:
The above solution worked for me for below issue. Thank you @raihan_siddiqui 

CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][NoWorkerInstanceAvailable] : Unable to deploy as the worker node is temporarily unavailable. Please try again after some time. If the issue persists, contact SAP.

entry.title: Candidate and Manager need to sign Offer Letter (without DocuSign nor AdobeSign)
entry.content:
We have a requirement to allow Manager to be the first recipient of offer letter for their approval by them providing their initial and date in the letter before sending to the candidate. Then the candidate also needs to provide their initial and date when accepting the offer.

Currently we cannot find a solution to allow this requirement. Our organization do not approve of AdobeSign nor Docusign to integrate as they only prefer to have the candidate/manager enter their name/date stamp in the document instead.

entry.title: Re: MRM Blog 3: Configuring Exchange Rate Updates in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content: @ReneSteen Did you activate the scope item 1S4 for automatic market rates?

entry.title: Re: Supplier Rating App in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Good Morning,

There are a lot of apps to evaluate suppliers, but more details would be needed.

But at the moment below you can find a few examples:

App - Overall Supplier Evaluation

More details you can find here:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2019')/S34

App - Supplier Evaluation By Quality (for Quality Notification) (Version 2)

More details you can find here:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3295A')/S34

App - Supplier Evaluation by Questionnaire (Version 2)

More details you can find here:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2234A')/S34

App - Supplier Evaluation by Quantity (Version 2)

More details you can find here:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1661A')/S34

App - Manage User-Defined Criteria for Supplier Evaluation

More details you can find here:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3812')/S34

App - Supplier Evaluation by Time (Version 2)

More details you can find here:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1664A')/S34

App - Purchase Order Average Delivery Time

More details you can find here:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1380')/S34

But as I said at the beginning it depends on the business case.

If you need more clarification please let me know.

Best Regards

Dariusz Kalmarczyk

entry.title: Re: RAP: Modify column values in Object page items table...
entry.content: I think the way is going through side effects. I found an example here  but it's on CAP not RAP. So we need to translate the Service cds document into behavior definition.

entry.title: Re: is it possible to get the payment medium flat file in...
entry.content:
Hello @Kavitha_141 

 A clean "table view" output is not standard but can be designed with a custom format or handled outside the system.

Options;

You can develop a Custom Payment Medium Format using the Manage Payment Formats app, where you structure the file closer to your needs (e.g., CSV with clear separators).
Alternatively, post-processing of the file externally (Example, with Excel or a script) can help present it in a readable table format.

SAP Help References:

  • Payment Medium Formats – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Manage Payment Formats App – SAP Help(select the country)

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: NF-e Technical Note 2023.004 v1.10: New Fields Added to Nota Fiscal
entry.content:
Hello, 

Also noticed that we do not have the XML LOGBR_NF_TO_XML_DOWNLOAD in our system. What is the SAP note that provide this XML creation ? 
Thanks in advance.

Regards, 

Fred Góes

entry.title: New enhancements to RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard
entry.content:
We are excited to announce significant enhancements to the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard, building upon the application's successful release in January this year. These new updates aim to provide a more comprehensive and insightful experience, empowering you to optimize your SAP S/4HANA system and achieving a clean core. 

**Improved Software Stack Assessment** 

The updated dashboard now offers a more robust software stack assessment, emphasizing the importance of maintaining the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack. By ensuring your system is up-to-date, you can leverage SAP-delivered innovations, mitigate security risks, and align with the clean core principles. This enhancement enables you to:  

  • Verify that your system is on the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack 
  • Install the latest feature package stack/support package stack to stay current with SAP innovations and security patches 

**Simplified Upgrade Readiness Check** 

Upgrading to SAP S/4HANA has never been more streamlined, thanks to the SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades. This innovative tool empowers you to assess your current system's readiness for a seamless upgrade to your desired target SAP S/4HANA release. With a single glance, the SAP Readiness Check card provides a comprehensive overview of the most critical findings from your system's latest assessment, enabling you to gauge your preparedness for the upgrade or estimate the necessary effort. 

**Enhanced Extensibility Information** 

To facilitate a better understanding of your system's extensibility, we have introduced enhanced information on separate software components using the ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development. This update helps you: 

  • Determine if a separate software component has been created for building extensions in Tier 1 
  • Gain insight into your system's extensibility architecture and identify potential areas for improvement 

**Unused Customer Objects: A Key Focus Area** 

The updated dashboard now provides a dedicated card to track Unused Customer Objects, which are extensions created outside of the SAP standard. Reducing the number of Unused Customer Objects is a crucial goal of the extensibility dimension. The card displays: 

  • The current share of Unused Customer Objects in your system 
  • The number of Unused Objects and Trackable Objects in your system 

**Execution of Customer Objects: A Measure of Business Process Reliance** 

To assess your efforts for future upgrades and adaptation, the dashboard now offers a card that measures the Execution of Customer Objects. This update helps you: 

  • Understand how heavily your business processes rely on executable customer objects 
  • Compare the frequency of executable customer object execution to the total execution of all executable objects in your system (including SAP, partner, and customer objects) 

**Introducing the Operations View** 

In addition to these enhancements, we are introducing a new view.  

The RISE with SAP Operations View is a dashboard available for RISE with SAP customers designed to deliver daily insights and historical data on system health-related key performance indicators, enabling organizations to efficiently align with the clean core compliance goals from the operations dimension. 

Several metrics are collected from SAP Cloud ALM applications for all your configured, productive  SAP S/4HANA systems and evaluated on a daily basis against specific thresholds. The results of these metric compliance calculations are aggregated and organized in KPIs and scores displayed in the overview and detailed pages.  

Metrics, thresholds and evaluation algorithms are shared ensuring a homogeneous compliance calculation across all RISE with SAP customers. 

***Key Features and Benefits*** 

The Operations View provides daily insights, featuring up to 30 days of historical data available until the day before, for the System Health score and its KPIs: Connectivity, Exceptions, Background Processing and Performance. 

  • Overview of System Health score for all your productive SAP S/4HANA systems (Cloud Private Edition and On Premise) 
  • System-specific view to identify and analyze metrics deviations responsible for each KPI 
  • Jump-in to standard SAP Cloud ALM applications to access the highest level of details 

Overview page of the Operations View Dashboard 

For more information about the Operations View dashboard, see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/operations-view on the SAP Help Portal and the “Introducing the new RISE with SAP Operations View Dashboard” on the SAP Community Blogs portal. 

These updates demonstrate our ongoing commitment to delivering a comprehensive and actionable RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. By leveraging these enhancements, you can further optimize your SAP S/4HANA system, achieve a clean core, and drive business success. More details can be found on our Help Portal page: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/rise-with-sap. 

If you don’t want to miss out any new feature, subscribe to the What’s New viewer:  What's New in SAP Cloud ALM | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SUM unable to see SAP_UI SAINT package
entry.content:
Hi

Assume you miss the ACP.
Look for SAP_UI====754 in Softwarecenter Download it and upload in SPAM.
After that repeat the Phase.

HTH

Volker

entry.title: Re: SAP TM – MM Integration “Indicator for Service-Based Invoice Verification”

entry.title: Re: Neue Mitglieder
entry.content: Vielen Dank und auch dir alles Gute 🙂

entry.title: Re: Down payment request exceeding the PO value
entry.content:
Hello Harish, 

This is the expected system behavior. 

When a Down Payment Requests is made, the Purchase Order number is filled in as a reference to the order. The Down Payment Requests is actually in the supplier account and is not tied to the Purchase Order, so the system does not issue a warning.

We have a KBA that provides more details about this behavior. Please see the information below:

3614465 - Down Payment Requests exceeding Purchase Order value

URL: https://me.sap.com/notes/3614465

Regards, 

Ihan

entry.title: Re: Issue in PO Price Determination After OMET Configurat...
entry.content:
Hello Chetan, 

 

Can you validate the OMET Configuration based upon following SAP KBA - 

2298917 - Error 06 235 when creating Purchase Order - SAP for Me

 

thanks,

entry.title: Automating UI5 testing: How to thrive with wdi5
entry.content:
Recently I built an extension app which featured a dynamic checklist for users to complete. Questions appear based on answers already given, and there are a large number of different scenarios, each with a different question set. Some questions are unique to a particular scenario, but many others are shared across scenarios.

In order to maintain my sanity I decided to set up automated testing. My concern was that changes and fixes made for one scenario could cause issues with another scenario.  With automated testing I can test all of them every time I make changes.

As I wanted to test at the UI-level I was looking for end-to-end testing, replicating a real user journey.  This is distinct from unit tests, for example, which cover a simple 'unit' like a function. 

I decided that wdi5 (pronounced vee-d-i-5) was a good fit for my requirements. It's a plugin for Webdriver.IO, a popular automation framework. Whereas Webdriver.IO is agnostic when it comes to UI technologies, wdi5 is optimised for use with SAPUI5 and OpenUI5.  It brings specific APIs which you can use to interact with your UI5 apps in your test scripts.

I thought I should share some of the things I learnt during this project. I do so not as an expert on automated testing in general or wdi5 in particular (I'm not!), but rather as experienced UI5 developer looking to make more use of automated testing in my day to day work. The perspective of the novice is different to that of subject matter experts, and the latter might not anticipate the questions and problems that the former face.

If you too are new to wdi5 you might not know that:

You can use wdi5 in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

wdi5 tests run in-browser. The browser could be on your own machine or it could be virtual.  If you're a BAS user you can install the Headless Testing Framework plugin and then run your wdi5 tests from within BAS. The tests are running in headless Firefox. If you take this approach you need to take care that the Firefox version listed in your Webdriver.IO config file (wdio.conf.ts) matches the one that BAS is using.  Every now and then SAP will upgrade the Firefox version in the plugin, and you will get an error stating 'Unable to find a matching set of capabilities'. When this happens just check what the new Firefox version is ("firefox -v" at the CLI) then update your config accordingly. NB you can use any browser with wdi5, I just used Firefox because it's easy to use in BAS

You might want to bail

I want to know as soon as any of my tests fail. By setting the bail property to true, execution will finish as soon as the first failure occurs.  You can set this in your config file (wdio.conf.ts).

It's easy to debug your tests

If you open the JavaScript Debug Terminal, and run your tests from there, processing will pause at the breakpoints you add in your test files. If you are using BAS you can do this from the Command Palette.

You should probably put the kettle on

My 35 tests take about an hour to run. This requires some planning, like starting them before you take your lunch break.  I did find that tweaking the tests can make a big difference.  Some wdi5 functions are faster than others.  For example, I found the getChildren method was quite slow, especially when the controls weren't visible on screen (and therefore not in the DOM). It's also possible to run tests in parallel.

 

Everything is asynchronous

In your tests the function calls are asynchronous, and you need to await each one.  This is true even if the corresponding UI5 function is synchronous e.g getText().

wdi5 ❤️ TypeScript

You can write your tests in TypeScript (i.e. as .ts not .js files). See here if you want to know why this is desirable.

You need to get scrolling

Your tests can only interact with screen controls that are shown on screen.  You need to use scrolling functions like scrollIntoView() or browser.scroll() to ensure that the control you need is visible.

It's easy to screenshot your errors

When a test fails the root cause isn't always obvious.  Was there an error?  Was the control we tried to interact with even visible on screen?  I love that you can always capture a screenshot if there was an error, so you can see exactly what the screen looked like at that moment.  I did this by calling the browser.screenshot() function in the afterTest handler.

 

If you are ready to write your first wdi5 tests, keep the points above in mind and check out these resources:

Resources

wdi5 GitHub Documentation

Use WDIO and WDI5 for testing UI5 apps

state of testing in UI5: OPA5, UIVeri5 and wdi5

UI5's unified test tech stack

Testing UI5 Apps with wdi5 - Zero to Hero to Continuous Integration

entry.title: Re: Unable to deply XPI Inspector with SUM
entry.content: Hi Symin, Follow the SAP note 1934940 to deploy the XPI inspector.

entry.title: Employee created via Data Migration Cockpit could not be used
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

 

We have created a few employees with Data Migration Cockpit.

However, these users could not be selected when trying to maitain them as partner functions (SE & ER) of BP Customer, or via people picker when configuring workflows.

It would be very kind of you to guide us on how to activate these employees, if there is any field missing in data migration template.

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Automating UI5 testing: How to thrive with wdi5
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"wdi5 tests run in-browser. The browser could be on your own machine or it could be virtual.  If you're a BAS user you can install the Headless Testing Framework plugin and then run your wdi5 tests from within BAS. The tests are running in headless Firefox. If you take this approach you need to take care that the Firefox version listed in your Webdriver.IO config file (wdio.conf.ts) matches the one that BAS is using.  Every now and then SAP will upgrade the Firefox version in the plugin, and you will get an error stating 'Unable to find a matching set of capabilities'. When this happens just check what the new Firefox version is ("firefox -v" at the CLI) then update your config accordingly. NB you can use any browser with wdi5, I just used Firefox because it's easy to use in BAS."

 

Thanks for your sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: How to increase direct ownership percentage when amou...
entry.content:
If you are using activity-based Consolidation of Investments (COI), follow the process outlined below.
This scenario is commonly referred to as a step acquisition, where a parent company increases its ownership percentage in a subsidiary either by making an additional investment or by acquiring additional ownership without any further investment.

High-Level Steps:
Post the ownership percentage change (increase or decrease) in the investment account. If applicable, include any additional investment amounts (e.g., a 2% increase in ownership).

Run the Group Share Calculation task to reflect the updated ownership percentages (e.g., from 90% to 92%).

Execute the COI task. This will generate the Consolidation of Investments (COI) documents.

Note: The attached screenshot shows a step acquisition with an additional investment. However, in your case, the ownership percentage is increasing without an additional investment.

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting SAP Group Reporting Data Collection

entry.title: S4/HANA Public Cloud Mass Change Task Lists and Operations
entry.content: What is the application or transaction for Mass Changing Data on Task Lists and their Operations in SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud?

entry.title: Create Product with Generic Material type from soap
entry.content:
I want to create product with Generic Material type by using the api. 

 

I have searched that we can create with "Product Master - Replicate from Client to SAP S/4HANA Cloud ". 

But when I create using that api, it shows below error message. 

My xml is like below. 

<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope" xmlns:glob="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global" xmlns:gdt="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal/GDT" xmlns:yy1="http://SAPCustomFields.com/YY1_">
<soap:Header/>
<soap:Body>
<glob:ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequestMessage>
<MessageHeader>
<ID>0894EF4591011EDAB4A9AE3D29FAD840</ID>
<UUID>0894ef45-9101-1eda-b4a9-ae3d29fad840</UUID>
<CreationDateTime>2025-06-25T10:23:00Z</CreationDateTime>
<SenderBusinessSystemID>ABC_910</SenderBusinessSystemID>
<RecipientBusinessSystemID>XYZ_002</RecipientBusinessSystemID>
</MessageHeader>
<ProductMDMReplicateRequestMessage>
<MessageHeader>
<ID>0894EF4591011EDAB4A9AE3D29FAF840</ID>
<UUID>0894ef45-9101-1eda-b4a9-ae3d29faf840</UUID>
<CreationDateTime>2025-06-25T10:23:00Z</CreationDateTime>
<SenderBusinessSystemID>ABC_910</SenderBusinessSystemID>
<RecipientBusinessSystemID>XYZ_002</RecipientBusinessSystemID>
</MessageHeader>
<Product>
<ProductInternalID>CANDY3</ProductInternalID>
<UnformattedProductInternalID>SOAP_DEMO_101</UnformattedProductInternalID>
<ProductTypeCode>FERT</ProductTypeCode>
<IndustrySectorCode>M</IndustrySectorCode>
<DivisionCode>00</DivisionCode>
<ProductGroup>R1161003</ProductGroup>
<BaseMeasureUnitCode>PC</BaseMeasureUnitCode>
<Description actionCode="04">
<Description languageCode="EN">Demo Product2</Description>
</Description>
</Product>
</ProductMDMReplicateRequestMessage>
</glob:ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequestMessage>
</soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>

 

Could you please help me guide with the xml code to create product data? 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Mass change for task lists and operations
entry.content:

Do you know if there is an application or transaction in SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud to Mass Change Task Lists and Operations? I can mass change fields and data within Manage Maintenance Items and Manage Maintenace Plans. But with Task Lists on the apps that I have the task lists do not show any check / tick boxes on the left of them and I can not see anywhere stating Mass Change?

entry.title: Re: Create Event Reasons
entry.content:
Hi @sfcrt 

Post creating new event reasons, Role based permission has to be enabled for the required role to showcase in the "Termination" or "Offboarding" screen. 

Can you check the offboarding event reason population, post enabling the required RBP?

Thanks 

Arun

entry.title: Re: Propogate a freshly calculated product cost as materi...

entry.content: You can directly copy a widget or even a page and paste into the other story, this will bring over calculations attached to widget.

entry.content: Transaction OVXC does not support alternatives such as batch input, BAPI, SM30, or LSMW.

entry.title: Non SAP System Integration with SAP GTS for compliance screening
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are checking the feasibility of integrating an External system with SAP GTS for master and transaction data.

Below API"s are known to us.

/SAPSLL/API_6850_SYNCH_MASS - API for Product master creation/change

/SAPSLL/API_1006_SYNCH_MASS - API for Business Partner master creation/change

/SAPSLL/API_6800_SYNCH_MASS - API for Custom document creation/change

We have CPI middleware for data transmission, and we would like to understand if there are any other Web services available for this.

If APIs are already there so what is an advantage of integrating CPI/PI to SAP GTS it will call for unnecessary configuration of Message type and whole associated set up in GTS.

Also if there are any web API's available you would know for Master and Transactional data?As per our understanding be it API or Web API its just medium to transmit data ,any pointers on this will be helpful.

For transaction data screening we need to configure elements like Logical system, ,logical system group, doc. type, item category etc as per my understanding anyone came across with any integration scenarion like this can can share there experience then it will be great.

Also one more observation when we are executing API 1006 manually there is response in the result however it is not seen under transaction BP or table BUT000/PNTBP.

 

Regards,

Dharmesh

entry.title: Re: account statement field in customer master data in sa...
entry.content:
Can you please in configuration path:

CBC => General Business Data => Business Partners => Business Partner Master Data => Basic Settings for Business Partners => Define Account Groups with Screen Layout (Customers)

Choose your groups (default CUST):

 

Go to details:

 

Double click on "Company Code data"

 

Double click on "Correspondence"

 

And check if "Account statement" is set to optional or required.

entry.title: Re: Ariba CIG : Split Text
entry.content: Since the split option for email is not achievable into a single field.Abap changes were done to resolve it

entry.title: Re: Maintain the Business Partner/ Supplier Name in multi...
entry.content:
Yes, It is possible to maintain business Partners in Multiple languages using International Address Versions. 
Use configuration activity Activate International Address Versions - Client-Specific - 103268

Follow below steps for configuring International Address Versions :

  1. Open 'Manage Your Solution' app.
  2. Navigate to 'Configure Your Solution'.
  3. Choose configuration activity Activate International Address Versions  Client-Specific - 103268
  4. Select configure 

Refer below documentation for more details - International Address Versions

Hi All, I also have same issue, where I cannot unhide the enhanced fields in RSA6, its giving error "OLTP error".

Regards,

Amit

entry.title: From ECC to Cloud: Your Essential SAP MM Guide for 2025 and Beyond
entry.content:
From ECC to Cloud: Your Essential SAP MM Guide for 2025 and Beyond

 

In the rapidly evolving world of SAP, staying current means embracing the transformative power of SAP Cloud. This blog brings you up to speed, explaining how the core principles of Materials Management (MM) have evolved within SAP's intelligent Business suite, highlighting the shift from traditional T-codes and tables to the user-centric Fiori experience and a simplified data model.

 

What is SAP MM (in the S/4HANA)?

 

SAP MM (Materials Management), now a core component within SAP S/4HANA, the next-generation intelligent ERP suite from SAP, continues to handle the critical functions of procurement, inventory management, material valuation, and vendor management. It ensures materials are available at the right quantity, at the right time, and at the right price, remaining the backbone of supply chain and logistics in enterprises.

While the fundamental business processes remain, SAP S/4HANA transforms how these processes are executed and how data is managed, driven by its in-memory HANA database and a simplified data model.

 

Why SAP MM in S/4HANA is Crucial for Business Operations

 

SAP MM in S/4HANA automates material flow, bridging the gap between departments and fostering real-time visibility and financial compliance. It streamlines:

  • Purchase requisitions and orders

  • Goods receipts and invoice verifications

  • Stock transfers and adjustments

  • Vendor evaluations and sourcing decisions

With deeper integration to SAP FI, CO, SD, and PP modules, and enhanced by intelligent technologies, SAP MM in S/4HANA empowers faster decision-making and more efficient operations.

 

Core Functionalities of SAP MM (with a Fiori Perspective)

 

The move to S/4HANA brings a strong emphasis on the Fiori experience. Here's how key MM functionalities are presented:

Feature

Description

Fiori Apps (Examples)

Purchasing

Manages Purchase Requisitions (PRs), Purchase Orders (POs), Requests for Quotations (RFQs), and Contracts.

"Manage Purchase Requisitions," "Manage Purchase Orders"

Inventory Mgmt.

Handles goods receipts, issues, and transfers, providing real-time stock visibility.

"Post Goods Receipt," "Stock - Single Material", Manage stock

Material Master

Central record for all materials, enriched with attributes for purchasing, inventory, and valuation.

"Manage Product Master Data" (classic T-codes like MM01/02/03 still available in some deployments)

Vendor Master

Maintains supplier information. Crucially, in S/4HANA, vendors are managed as Business Partners.

"Manage Supplier Master Data" (or T-code BP)

Invoice Verif.

Reconciles purchase orders, goods receipts, and vendor invoices.

"Manage Supplier Invoices," "Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced"

Material Valuation

Handles pricing and costing based on movement types, with a simplified data model.

Manage Material Valuations

 

Important SAP MM Tables (and their S/4HANA Evolution)

 

While many traditional tables still exist (often as compatibility views), SAP S/4HANA introduces significant simplifications and consolidations for improved performance and a "clean core."

 

Material Master Tables

 

These remain largely consistent, with underlying architecture optimized for HANA:

  • MARA: General material data

  • MAKT: Material descriptions

  • MARC: Plant-specific material data

  • MBEW: Material valuation

  • MVKE: Sales organization data (relevant for sales)

 

Vendor Master Tables (The Business Partner Revolution)

 

This is a major change in S/4HANA. All vendors (and customers) are now managed under the Business Partner (BP) concept. Traditional LFA1, LFB1, and LFM1 tables are still populated via Customer/Vendor Integration (CVI), but the BP is the leading object.

  • BUT000: Business Partner: General data I (The central BP table)

  • LFA1: General vendor data (linked to BP via CVI)

  • LFB1: Company code-specific vendor data (linked to BP via CVI)

  • LFM1: Purchasing organization-specific vendor data (linked to BP via CVI)

 

Purchasing Tables

 

These tables remain foundational:

  • EKKO: Purchase order header

  • EKPO: Purchase order item

  • EBAN: Purchase requisition

  • EKBE: History per purchasing document

  • EKKN: Account assignment in PO

 

Inventory Management Tables (The Rise of MATDOC)

 

The biggest simplification here is the MATDOC table, which consolidates data previously spread across MKPF (material document header) and MSEG (material document item). While MKPF and MSEG still exist for compatibility, MATDOC is the go-to for S/4HANA.

  • MATDOC: Material Document (Combined Header and Item) - This single table replaces MKPF and MSEG for new material documents.

  • MKPF: Document header for material movements (Still present, but data primarily accessed via MATDOC)

  • MSEG: Material document line items (Still present, but data primarily accessed via MATDOC)

 

Universal Journal (ACDOCA)

 

While not directly an MM table, the Universal Journal (ACDOCA) is a fundamental innovation. It consolidates financial postings from all modules, including MM (e.g., goods receipts, invoice verification), into a single table. This provides real-time financial insights and eliminates reconciliations.

 

Essential SAP MM Reports (and their Fiori Equivalents)

 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the emphasis is on SAP Fiori apps for a role-based, intuitive, and highly analytical user experience. While many classic T-codes might still be available in some S/4HANA on-premise or private cloud deployments, Fiori is the future.

 

Procurement Reports

 

Classic T-Code

Description

Recommended Fiori App(s)

ME2N

Purchase orders by PO number

Manage Purchase Orders, Monitor Purchase Order Items

ME2M

Purchase orders by material

Monitor Purchase Order Items

ME80FN

General analysis of PO

Purchase Order Analytics, Supplier Performance

ME5A

List display of purchase requisitions

Manage Purchase Requisitions

ME2L

Purchase orders by vendor

Manage Purchase Orders, Monitor Supplier Deliveries

 

Inventory Reports

 

Classic T-Code

Description

Recommended Fiori App(s)

MB52

Stock overview

Stock - Single Material, Stock - Multiple Materials

MB5B

Stock on key date

Stock - Single Material (with key date filter)

MB51

Material document list

Material Document List (or similar Fiori analytical apps)

MC.9

Material analysis

Overview Inventory Management, Material Documents Analytics

 

Vendor Evaluation Reports

 

Classic T-Code

Description

Recommended Fiori App(s)

ME61

Maintain vendor evaluation

Operational Supplier Evaluation, Manage Supplier Ratings

ME63

Display vendor evaluation

Operational Supplier Evaluation, Supplier Performance

ME6H

Vendors without evaluation

Supplier Performance, Monitor Supplier Deliveries

 

Pro Tips for Using SAP MM in S/4HANA Efficiently

 

  • Embrace Fiori First: Fiori apps are your primary interface in cloud deployments. Get familiar with the Fiori Launchpad and its powerful analytical capabilities.

  • Leverage Embedded Analytics: S/4HANA's embedded analytics (often within Fiori apps) provide real-time dashboards and insights, reducing the need for separate reporting tools.

  • Utilize Dynamic Selections & Filters: Fiori apps offer extensive filtering options by document type, plant, material group, vendor, and more to refine your data.

  • Automate with Intelligent Technologies: Explore how S/4HANA leverages AI, Machine Learning, and Robotic Process Automation (RPA) within MM processes (e.g., automated invoice processing, predictive analytics for stock levels).

  • Understand Business Partner (BP): This is fundamental. All master data for vendors (and customers) is now managed through the BP.

  • Consider SAP Ariba Integration: For strategic sourcing and procure-to-pay, SAP Ariba integrates seamlessly with S/4HANA MM, offering enhanced collaboration with suppliers.

 

Cloud-Specific Considerations

 

If you're moving to the cloud, keep these in mind:

  • Extensibility: Direct table access (like SE16N) is often restricted in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Extensibility is achieved via Fiori apps, custom fields, and APIs, adhering to the "clean core" principle.

  • Upgrade Cycles: Be aware of the regular, mandatory upgrade cycles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ensuring you stay up-to-date with the latest features.

  • API-Driven Integration: Focus on standard APIs for integrations with other systems, as direct database access is not the standard cloud approach.

 

Conclusion

 

Mastering SAP MM in the SAP S/4HANA era means more than just knowing tables and T-codes. It's about understanding the shift to a Fiori-first user experience, the simplified data model (especially with MATDOC and Business Partner), and the power of intelligent technologies.

By leveraging the right SAP Fiori apps, understanding the underlying data structure, and embracing the capabilities of SAP S/4HANA, you can significantly enhance your efficiency, streamline procurement and inventory processes, and drive smarter decision-making across your supply chain in 2025 and beyond.

Ready to take your SAP MM skills to the next level in the cloud era? Share your thoughts or questions in the comments below!

entry.title: Re: Filtering WRTTP = 04 records from ACDOCA
entry.content: Hi Sir, I am also into a similar situation .Can you please help me if you find the solution on your question

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 28: Terraform Destroy
entry.content:
In this video, we demonstrate how to easily destroy all resources created with Terraform using a single command. Terraform tracks the state of all created resources, allowing for easy destruction by running `terraform destroy`. The process analyzes the state and removes all resources like sub accounts and directories, returning the environment to its initial state. This feature is useful for creating temporary environments, such as sandboxes for business hours, and ensures that everything can be quickly removed at the end of the day, making it ideal for testing or short-term projects.

Takeaways

  • Terraform maintains the state of what it creates, such as sub accounts and directories.
  • The terraform destroy command analyzes the current state to determine what needs to be destroyed.
  • Running terraform destroy will identify all created resources and prepare them for removal.
  • The script will display what resources are going to be destroyed, such as sub accounts and directories.
  • Once you confirm with 'yes', Terraform will begin destroying the resources it previously created.
  • Terraform can be used to create and destroy temporary environments, like a sandbox for business use during work hours.
  • Destruction may take a few minutes, depending on the complexity of the created resources.
  • After the destroy command completes, everything that was created will be gone, as if nothing ever happened.
  • Terraform's destroy functionality makes it easy to remove all resources without manual intervention.
  • The process demonstrates the convenience of using Terraform to automate the lifecycle of environments, from creation to destruction.
  • Terraform can be used for temporary setups that only need to exist during business hours and can be recreated the next day.

entry.title: How to download a file from SFTP Server in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
Requirement:- Pick the file from SFTP server and create a sales order ion SAP

Issue:- We are not able to access/download the file from the server although we are able to access the server.

Can someone please help here what we are missing?

entry.title: Re: Email channel profile avatar
entry.content:
@Khayakazi wrote:

How can I fix the issue where emails sent from SAP Service Cloud Version 2 show a question-mark avatar and the label ‘via amazonses.com’ in Gmail and Outlook? I want recipients to see the sender’s initials (or avatar) instead and remove the ‘via amazonses.com’ tag on all customer emails sent from C4C Version 2.

To fix emails from SAP Service Cloud Version 2 showing a question-mark avatar and 'via amazonses.com', you primarily need to configure SPF (Sender Policy Framework) and DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) for your sending domain. This involves contacting SAP Support to get the necessary DKIM keys and instructions, then updating your domain's DNS records with these keys and an SPF entry that includes SAP's sending servers (include:_spf.cmail.ondemand.com). Finally, activate the DKIM profile with SAP. Implementing DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance) further enhances trust. Once these email authentication protocols are correctly set up and verified, email clients like Gmail and Outlook will recognize your domain as the legitimate sender, remove the 'via amazonses.com' tag, and often display your sender's initials or avatar, leading to a more professional appearance for your customer communications.

entry.title: Cumulative/YTD load for FI to Group Reporting Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
We have a scenario where the Finance team performs its financial consolidation once a year at year-end. During the year they keep all the FI periods open and post corrections as needed in the periods (for example fixed asset depreciation to the respective period).
The organization migrated from SAP ECC 6.0 EHP08 to S/4HANA 2023 FP01.
Now we are migrating from EC-CS to Group Reporting.
No GRDC.
Previously, there was a custom program that created a YTD file for import from FI to EC-CS so that only one cumulative import was required at year-end.

Is there a way to do the same with Group Reporting?
Realignment with YTD may be an option. There are many Cons units and a large data volume.
B) Maybe frequent scheduled import for each period between


Regards,
Eyal

entry.title: Re: Maintain Ship-to-party parameter in SAP PO Template
entry.content: Hi Chuma, it seems that I can't find 101017 in my CBC site. @Chuma

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Level Hierarchy - Help please
entry.content: You need to import your model (in your case as CSV file), that would already contain levels of your hierarchy. Then you create an hierarchy within your model in SAC according to the instruction in the video.

entry.title: SAP CPQ: Displaying Attributes Based on Sales Organization in Configurable Products
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m working with Configurable Products in SAP CPQ and looking to conditionally display specific product attributes to sales users based on the Sales Organization assigned to the quote or user context.

Here’s what I’m trying to achieve:

  • In the product configuration screen, only a subset of attributes should be visible or editable depending on the Sales Organization (e.g., Sales Org A sees a different set than Sales Org B).

  • This should ideally be dynamic, so that the visibility or behavior of attributes changes based on the sales org context without duplicating the product or maintaining separate versions.

I’ve considered the following approaches but haven’t found a clean solution yet:

  • Attribute visibility rules: but they don’t seem to support Sales Organization directly.

Has anyone implemented a similar setup? I’d appreciate any suggestions, best practices, or pointers to documentation or scripting examples.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to Reterive back standard DPC_Ext class after deleting accidently.
entry.content: To recover a standard DPC_EXT class (Data Provider Class Extension) in SAP that was accidentally deleted, you can recreate the class with the same name and then retrieve its previous version using Version Management in SE24 or SE80.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori tools 2506 update – Open source, faster app creation, adaptation boost, AI enhancement
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"We also added support for creating local annotation files within adaptation projects. Whether you're defining new views or tweaking behavior, you now benefit from the same intelligent editing experience provided by the Language Server Protocol (LSP), including auto-completion, code snippets, and quick fixes—making it easier than ever to stay within the Fiori elements design guidelines."

 

Thanks for your sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Combining of interpretation algorithm 001 with strings
entry.content:
I am trying to add string rules that search for account numbers and assign posting rules within interpretation algorithm 001 (OT83). However, the system does not seem to accept this configuration—it either routes the strings to the "adv" field or misinterprets customer numbers as vendor numbers.

Is there a way to configure this setup correctly so that the string rules work with algorithm 001 and correctly identify the customer accounts and posting rules?

Posting rule should work

It's posting for customer, but somehow the system reads it as vendor.

entry.title: Re: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508
entry.content:
Hi Alan,

RASD is updated with 2508 content as of June 23rd. 

Thanks,
Prasanth

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 FilterBar with SmartVariantManagement
entry.content: @yogesh_galphade, if your system is not very old, at this point I would rather suggest you to use SmartTable instead. It is easier and it is pretty customizable, allows you to even have custom filters or table columns. The approach I have implemented in this blog was useful in ERP (pre-HANA) systems where the smart controls were a bit more rigid.

entry.title: Re: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508
entry.content: Thanks a lot

entry.title: Re: Microsoft AutoGen v0.6.1 with SAP AI Core
entry.content: Thanks Shivang for sharing the information. Its very helpful.

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting Process in Production Planning - SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Priya,

This is a such a document you have provided with very detailed explanation. Thank you for sharing the knowledge .

Theja| QM consultant

entry.title: About KSS2(Split Costs - Cost Centers - Actual) in public cloud
entry.content:
Dear all：

In S4hana GUI, we use KSS2 to allocate the actual cost of primary cost elements in the production Cost Centers to activity types, and then calculate the actual unit price of activity types through KSII. But in the Public Cloud, I think
Execute Actual Price Calculation corresponds to KSII, but I cannot find the corresponding application for KSS2.
Moreover, there is no description of KSS2 in Public Cloud scope item 33Q. Is the KSS2 step no longer available in the public cloud, and can we simply execute Execute Actual Price Calculation? How are the primary cost elements of the production Cost Centers's production costs allocated to activity types?

Thank you & best regards

Wang

entry.title: Follow-Up Material Not Considered In MRP Run
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are discontinuing X packaging material from 01.07.2025 and we have maintained Y material as follow-up material in material master data. Currently we have stock of 8000 units for X material which covers the requirement till the date of 18.07.2025, so whatever requirements after this date should go on follow up material Y, but system is not creating any requirements for Y material when we run the MRP. We have created variant to run MRP which is based on the MRP controller, so the MRP controller for the Y material is same as we given in variant, still it's not creating any requirement. but when we run MRP only for Y material it's creating the requirements in system. I'm not understanding why it's happening like this. Can you pls provide your expertise here. Master data is also same for X & Y. Only issue is that when we run MRP for all packaging material that time it's not creating any requirement on follow up material 'Y' and when run MRP individually only for 'Y' material then it's creating the requirements.

entry.title: Required more information regarding Hibernate feature in Cloud Portal
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are seeing new feature called Hibernate in our CCV2 Portal. We want to know what is Hibernate, what is it's purpose and how to use it.

Please Provide us some documentation.

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Required more information regarding Hibernate feature...
entry.content: Please check the official documentation regarding this feature: Hibernate Environments.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Index on Global Temporary Table in S...
entry.content: Just found the question by accidentally, with my findings, it's not supported to create index on global temporary column table(default table type), but if we create a global temporary row table, we could create index on it.

entry.title: PO Goods Receipt Exception Task Automation in SAP ByDesignPO Goods Receipt Exception Task Automat
entry.content: Hi i try to extend an BO and make some pdi developments via sdk app i try to create a .xbo for the trigger of Inbound Logistics - Purchase Orders post good receit on save Find the trigger for receiving a purchase order to the warehouse and creating the INBOUND DELIVERY as a result. i try to extend ConfirmedInboundDelivery but i am not sure if this is the right place : my goal is to create .xbo there check some validations like: 1.1. When a warehouse user posts a Purchase Order Goods Receipt (GR), Gauzy requires follow-up in cases where certain exceptions occur (per line): a. Over-Quantity Receipt – The total received quantity is greater than the ordered quantity on the PO line by more than 5%. b. Early Delivery – The receipt is posted before the delivery date that was defined on the PO line by more than month and year. then we have to check the Target logistic area id and do some validations .... the main request is that you try to answer me what is tje correct .xbo that i need to extend in order to check this trigger process i attached a screen shot of the window that i need to exctend

entry.title: Re: Data Ownership missing
entry.content: Hi, Please provide screenshot of issue faced and version of SAP Business One in which you have issue.

entry.title: Re: Resource type in Operation Activity in SAP DMC (Publi...
entry.content: i confirm Manuel answer! he comes just few min before me. Just a littel comment for point 2 : not so easy to use XSLT to overwrite resource type in master routing cause ERP usually trasmit workcenter, from workcenter you need to find resource (only 1 to 1 relation) and from resource find the resource type.

entry.title: Re: Cannot change Document Owner in SAP Business One vers...
entry.content:
Hi kedalenechong 

Could you please check if customer is using Data Ownership ?
if yes then According to the definition the only one who can change owner in the owner field in the header is a manager to himself or to his subordinates.

If an employee has no subordinates he will not be able to change it.

Kind Regards
Nidhi 
SAP Support

entry.title: Issue with deployment of SAP UI5 application from BAS to BSP ABAP Repository.
entry.content:
Hi colleagues,

I'm unable to deploy UI5 application to ABAP Repository from Business Application Studio. I'm getting below errors:

Below is the deployment config:

Regards,

Saksham

entry.title: Re: Disable Standard Delete Button in VA02 for All Users...
entry.content: Wondering whether you discussed this requirement with your Security team.  I am sure, with an authorization object for deletion, this can be controlled for va02 either for specific users or for all

entry.title: About depreciation Area of 20
entry.content:
Dear all ：
In our system's Chinese chart of depreciation , a depreciation Area of 20 "cost accounting depreciation" has been set for  assets, and it have also been set for posting depreciation. However, an error was reported during the depreciation process. error is shown: "Account 'X-Y' not allowed to be defined as reconciliation account" - Message no. AC309. I followed the advice provided in SAP Note 1888597, -change message: AC309 from "Error" to "Warning" using transaction OBA5. Depreciation was carried out again and it was found that depreciation area 01 can be posted, but depreciation area 20 still reported the same error.
Does anyone know how to use the depreciation area of 20"cost accounting depreciation"? Does it need to be actually posted?
Why did I change this error message to a warning in OBA5, but it still reports a red light?
Thank you and best regards!
Wang

entry.title: Change Output determination in settlement document
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I have a question. I'm trying to print a settlement document using an output type created in NACE, but when I try to add the message, I get this screen:

But I would like this view to appear so I can add the output type that I already have with the SMARTFORM:

 

How can I change this view?

entry.title: Re: Governance membership "admin" excludes "collaborator"...
entry.content:
Hey Adriana,

thanks for the reply.

i'm only speaking about Governance. At the Docu i found which rights you have as Collaborator and Admin. There they are separated from each other and not cobined in the role of Admin.

For me that as well does not sound correct and that was the reason i reached out first. Because with that docu it looked like being right.

I will create a ticket.

Greetings

Daniel

entry.title: Good Issue check upon Shipment Completion
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

May I know if there is any standard configuration in SAP that allows activation of a check on the GI (Goods Issue) status during shipment completion?

I am aware that there is a picking check available in the "Define Shipment Type" configuration, but I have not been able to locate a similar control for PGI. Is there a way to enable this functionality without implementing enhancements through user exits?

Your guidance on this would be greatly appreciated.

Regards,

 

Zaida Ishak

entry.title: Re: Issue in MIGO Accounting Entry
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Type for input parameter for XML string
entry.content: Try it first, then ask a question if it doesn't work. But I don't get why you want one parameter of type XML instead of normal parameters.

entry.title: Re: Type for input parameter for XML string
entry.content:
Hello Sandra,

I have tried it but the caller receives an exception.

 

Actually, the caller of the web service sends an XML message and I want to import it, transform it using a Z simple transformation and then process it.

entry.title: Root Certificate Authority (CA) Update (*businessbydesign.cloud.sap) & (*sapbydesign.com)
entry.content:
Background

As part of the evolving landscape of internet security, compliance requirements, and our ongoing commitment to delivering secure and trusted services, Cloud Integration (ISUITE) will be updating the Root Certificate Authority (CA) used for load balancer certificates in both design-time and runtime environments.


What Is Changing?

To maintain compliance with the latest cryptographic standards, we are transitioning from the DigiCert Global Root G2 certificate to the DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 certificate.

The DigiCert G5 Root represents the next generation of certificate infrastructure, leveraging a stronger SHA-256 signature algorithm (as opposed to the older SHA-1), providing enhanced protection and aligning with current industry best practices.

 

What Do I Need to Do?

If your systems:

  • Implement certificate pinning, or
  • Explicitly trust only certain root certificates (e.g., DigiCert Global Root G2)

You must ensure that the new DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 certificate is added to your trusted certificate store before the migration begins to avoid any disruption in service communication.

 

Certificate Comparison

Below is a summary of the changes:

Certificate AuthorityNew (G5) CertificatesCurrent (G2) Certificates
DigiCertDigiCert G5 TLS RSA4096 SHA384 2021 CA1 (Intermediate)

DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA256 2020 CA1 (Intermediate)

 DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 (Root)DigiCert Global Root G2 (Root)


Download new certificate

Below are the steps to download new certificate:

    • Kindly click on download link. You will be redirected to Digi Cert Website, here ensure Combined Certificate Files is set as shown below.

 

    • Click download as shown below.

    • A zip file by name: star_businessbydesign_cloud_sap_1299509218 would be downloaded.
    • Please unzip this file and we can see required certificate: star_businessbydesign_cloud_sap.crt as shown below.

 

FAQs

1) What are these certificates used for?
These certificates are used for the SSL/TLS handshake that any system using the ‘secure’ protocol does before allowing connection to/from the system. In our case, SAP Business ByDesign uses the ‘secure’ HTTPS protocol and hence the SSL handshake is must for any system to connect to these URLs.

2) Are the new certificates known to modern web browsers?
DigiCert Root Certificates are automatically recognized by all common web browsers, mobile devices, and mail clients, therefore for browser scenarios there is nothing to do. The same is true if one relies on the standard sapjvm trust list.
The CA root certificate is included in:

    • SAP JVM patch level 8.1.035 or 7.1.054
    • Cloud Foundry buildpack SAP-Java (sap_java_buildpack) version 1.6.15

3) How do I download or install the certificate?
You must have admin access to the server where you need to install the certificate. If you do not have access to your company’s SSL server, notify your IT team and provide them the respective certificate download link from the above table.

4) How do Import Single Certificate in SAP CPI Key Store?
Follow the steps mentioned in the link.

5) How to check the certificate in my browser trust list?
Navigate to chrome://settings and scroll down to ‘Advanced’.

    • Under “Privacy and Security,” click “Manage Certificates.”
    • On the popup that was launched, select “Trusted Root Certification Authorities’. The certificate will be displayed there.


6) How to import the certificate into my browser?
Procedure

    • Open chrome browser.
    • Click Customize and control Google Chrome button in the upper right corner.
    • Choose Settings. …
    • Under Privacy and security section, click More. …
    • Click Manage certificates, The new window will appear. …
    • Choose Trusted Root Certification Authorities tab.
    • Click Import. …
    • In the opened window, click Next.


7) I notice a discrepancy in the validity start date and end date mentioned in this knowledge article table and my downloaded certificate. What does this indicate?
Sometimes, due to time zone difference, you may see a different date in the downloaded certificate. There is no impact on the certificate update activity due to this. You will be renewing the certificate well in advance, before the certificate expiry date.

 

Please do share feedback and your thoughts in the comment section below.
You can also refer SAP Business ByDesign environment.
Topic page: (https://community.sap.com/topics/business-bydesign)
Post and answer questions (https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000691)
Read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000691/)

entry.title: Root Certificate Authority (CA) Update (*businessbydesign.sapcloud.cn) & (*sapbyd.cn)
entry.content:
Background

As part of the evolving landscape of internet security, compliance requirements, and our ongoing commitment to delivering secure and trusted services, Cloud Integration (ISUITE) will be updating the Root Certificate Authority (CA) used for load balancer certificates in both design-time and runtime environments.


What Is Changing?

To maintain compliance with the latest cryptographic standards, we are transitioning from the DigiCert Global Root G2 certificate to the DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 certificate.

The DigiCert G5 Root represents the next generation of certificate infrastructure, leveraging a stronger SHA-256 signature algorithm (as opposed to the older SHA-1), providing enhanced protection and aligning with current industry best practices.

 

What Do I Need to Do?

If your systems:

  • Implement certificate pinning, or
  • Explicitly trust only certain root certificates (e.g., DigiCert Global Root G2)

You must ensure that the new DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 certificate is added to your trusted certificate store before the migration begins to avoid any disruption in service communication.

 

Certificate Comparison

Below is a summary of the changes:

Certificate AuthorityNew (G5) CertificatesCurrent (G2) Certificates
DigiCertDigiCert G5 TLS RSA4096 SHA384 2021 CA1 (Intermediate)

DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA256 2020 CA1 (Intermediate)

 DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 (Root)DigiCert Global Root G2 (Root)


Download new certificate

Below are the steps to download new certificate:

    • Kindly click on download link. You will be redirected to Digi Cert Website, here ensure Combined Certificate Files is set as shown below.

 

    • Click download as shown below.

    • A zip file by name: star_businessbydesign_sapcloud_cn_1299500449 would be downloaded.
    • Please unzip this file and we can see required certificate: star_businessbydesign_sapcloud_cn.crt as shown below.

 

FAQs

1) What are these certificates used for?
These certificates are used for the SSL/TLS handshake that any system using the ‘secure’ protocol does before allowing connection to/from the system. In our case, SAP Business ByDesign uses the ‘secure’ HTTPS protocol and hence the SSL handshake is must for any system to connect to these URLs.

2) Are the new certificates known to modern web browsers?
DigiCert Root Certificates are automatically recognized by all common web browsers, mobile devices, and mail clients, therefore for browser scenarios there is nothing to do. The same is true if one relies on the standard sapjvm trust list.
The CA root certificate is included in:

    • SAP JVM patch level 8.1.035 or 7.1.054
    • Cloud Foundry buildpack SAP-Java (sap_java_buildpack) version 1.6.15

3) How do I download or install the certificate?
You must have admin access to the server where you need to install the certificate. If you do not have access to your company’s SSL server, notify your IT team and provide them the respective certificate download link from the above table.

4) How do Import Single Certificate in SAP CPI Key Store?
Follow the steps mentioned in the link.

5) How to check the certificate in my browser trust list?
Navigate to chrome://settings and scroll down to ‘Advanced’.

    • Under “Privacy and Security,” click “Manage Certificates.”
    • On the popup that was launched, select “Trusted Root Certification Authorities’. The certificate will be displayed there.


6) How to import the certificate into my browser?
Procedure

    • Open chrome browser.
    • Click Customize and control Google Chrome button in the upper right corner.
    • Choose Settings. …
    • Under Privacy and security section, click More. …
    • Click Manage certificates, The new window will appear. …
    • Choose Trusted Root Certification Authorities tab.
    • Click Import. …
    • In the opened window, click Next.


7) I notice a discrepancy in the validity start date and end date mentioned in this knowledge article table and my downloaded certificate. What does this indicate?
Sometimes, due to time zone difference, you may see a different date in the downloaded certificate. There is no impact on the certificate update activity due to this. You will be renewing the certificate well in advance, before the certificate expiry date.

 

Please do share feedback and your thoughts in the comment section below.
You can also refer SAP Business ByDesign environment.
Topic page: (https://community.sap.com/topics/business-bydesign)
Post and answer questions (https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000691)
Read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000691/)

entry.title: Re: Restrict Data Point Comments visibility to individual...
entry.content:
Hi, thanks a lot for your help!

I would like to know how can I filter comments entered by other users?

Best regards,
Elad
